componentDescription: Charts renders data visualizations — line, bar, pie, and area charts. Use for analytics dashboards, trend displays, and data comparison views. For simple metric cards with sparklines, use StatCard instead.
features: Four chart types: LINE, BAR, PIE, AREA
Header slot for chart title and controls

usageExamples.title: Monthly revenue line chart
usageExamples.description: Line chart showing revenue trend over the past 12 months.
usageExamples.code:
Monthly Revenue

} />

usageExamples.title: Category distribution pie chart
usageExamples.description: Pie chart showing distribution of support tickets by category.
usageExamples.code: Tickets by Category} />

data.llmContext: Required. Array of data objects. Each object should have consistent keys across all entries. The chart infers series from the data structure. For multi-series charts, include multiple value keys in each object.
data.propDescription: Required. Array of data objects for the chart

chartType.llmContext: LINE is best for trends over time (revenue, users, traffic). BAR is best for comparing discrete categories or periods side-by-side. PIE is best for showing composition/distribution as parts of a whole (use sparingly, max 5-6 segments). AREA is like LINE but emphasizes volume — good for cumulative metrics.
chartType.propDescription: Type of chart visualization to render

chartHeaderSlot.llmContext: React node rendered above the chart. Use for chart title, time period selector, legend, or export button. Provides context without being part of the chart canvas itself.
chartHeaderSlot.propDescription: React node rendered above the chart area

xAxisLabel.llmContext: Label for the horizontal axis. Describe what the x-axis represents (e.g., 'Month', 'Date', 'Category'). Required for LINE, BAR, and AREA charts for clarity.

yAxisLabel.llmContext: Label for the vertical axis. Describe the metric and include units where relevant (e.g., 'Revenue ($)', 'Users', 'Conversion Rate (%)').
